# Anti-Patterns — What Suppresses Creativity

**Anti-Pattern 1: Standardized Testing. Testing kills creativity by rewarding one right answer.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Passive Learning. Sitting and listening is not learning.**

**Case: The Drill. Repetitive exercises teach nothing about creative thinking.**

**Case: The Rubric. Over-specifying the outcome limits creative exploration.**

**Case: The Fear. Children who are afraid of being wrong stop taking risks.**

**Case: The Screen Time. Passive screen time is different from creative screen time.**
---

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

**Case: The Prescription. Telling children exactly what to make kills creativity.**
**Case: The Competition. Over-emphasizing competition discourages collaboration.**
**Case: The Perfection. Expecting perfect results on the first try inhibits experimentation.**
**Case: The Isolation. Learning alone without peers limits growth.**
**Case: The Screen. Passive consumption of media is not creative learning.**
**Case: The Right Answer. In creativity, there is no one right answer. Multiple answers are the goal.**
**Case: The Grade. Grading creative work contradicts the spirit of creativity.**
**Case: The Template. Pre-made templates can help but can also limit creativity.**
**Case: The Evaluation. If you must evaluate, evaluate process, not just product.**
